summary: run_query_pipeline() correctly chains refine_intent -> select_top_k -> expand_insufficient_topics -> combine_and_cap -> get_file -> synthesize_answer with every call site's positional/keyword arguments verified byte-for-byte against the real, already-verified (#22/#23/#24) function signatures -- no contract mismatches found. The DI-vs-real-wiring scope decision (injecting search_candidates/graph_neighbors/get_file as callables, and api/main.go's notImplementedPipeline stand-in) is legitimate and consistent with topic_selector.py's own pre-existing disclosed gap and the proto's actual RPC surface (SearchCandidates/GraphNeighbors/GetFile all exist in proto/hivemind.proto but have no client-side invocation wired from agents/query/ or api/ yet). The Go /query route, its 6 subtests, and the 3 Python pipeline tests are all substantive (real dataclass fixtures and a real LLMClient ABC subclass, not loose stubs). All tests pass, full regressions clean, lint/vet clean. Two non-blocking gaps found: (1) the acceptance criterion 'exposed via api/'s /query route' is satisfied only in the narrow 'reachable, structurally wired' sense -- an end user hitting POST /query today always gets a 500 with a not-implemented error, which is defensible for 4.6.1 alone but worth flagging since 4.6.2 depends on it; (2) GetFileFn's chosen (path, content) return shape has no matching field in proto's existing GetFileResponse (which only carries content + version, no path), a latent reconciliation gap for whoever wires get_file for real.

requirements_conformance.evidence: Issue #25 subtask 4.6.1 acceptance criteria: 'single pipeline function/class chains all three agents in order reachable through api/ gateway's /query HTTP route.' run_query_pipeline() exists as the single chaining function (agents/query/pipeline.py:130-236) and api/routes/query.go registers POST /query via RegisterRoutes(), invoked from api/main.go's newMux(). Both literal requirements (single function, route existence/reachability) are met.

architecture_conformance.evidence: docs/LLD/query-agent.md's 'Pipeline order' section (query -> intent_refiner -> topic_selector (+SearchCandidates/GraphNeighbors) -> synthesizer -> answer) matches pipeline.py's call order exactly. docs/HLD.md's API Gateway -> gRPC -> Python ML/Agent Service boundary is honored: no RPC for invoking the Python query pipeline exists yet in proto/hivemind.proto's service HiveMind (verified: only PutSegment/GetFile/ReadPartial/GraphNeighbors/SearchCandidates/ProposeSplit/PutEdge/PutEntity/LookupEntity are defined), so api/main.go's notImplementedPipeline stand-in returning a clear error, rather than fabricating a network call, is the architecturally honest choice and mirrors the QueryPipeline interface's own disclosed-gap doc comment.

contract_correctness.evidence: Verified against real signatures (not assumed): refine_intent(query, history, llm_client, *, model, temperature, max_tokens, timeout) -> IntentRefinerResult (agents/query/intent_refiner.py:214-223) called with matching positional args at pipeline.py. search_candidates(intent.refined_intent, max_candidates) matches SearchCandidatesFn = Callable[[str, int], Sequence[TopicCandidate]] (topic_selector.py:93). select_top_k(candidates, k=k) and combine_and_cap(selected, expansions, k=k) match topic_selector.py:96-100,378-390. expand_insufficient_topics(selected, graph_neighbors, hops=hops, ratio=ratio) matches topic_selector.py:269-281. synthesize_answer(intent.refined_intent, intent.query_type, intent.entities, selected_markdown, llm_client, *, model, temperature, max_tokens, timeout) -> SynthesizerResult matches synthesizer.py:274-285 exactly (positional order: refined_intent, query_type, entities, selected_markdown, llm_client). No field-name or type mismatches found in any call site.

scope_boundary_reasonableness.evidence: topic_selector.py:342-343's own pre-existing comment ('Mapping file_ids back to file content for the synthesizer prompt is left to a later subtask (out of scope here)') independently confirms the GetFileFn injection point is a genuine, previously-disclosed gap this subtask is explicitly scoped to close only partially (via DI, not real wiring). GetFile/GraphNeighbors/SearchCandidates RPCs all already exist in proto/hivemind.proto -- consistent with the stated convention that the proto messages exist but no client invocation code has been built in agents/query/ yet.
scope_boundary_reasonableness.comment: Acceptance criterion's phrase 'reachable through api/ gateway's /query HTTP route' is satisfied literally (route exists, is registered, responds), but produces a 500 for every real request today via notImplementedPipeline. This is a reasonable scope boundary for 4.6.1 in isolation (matches the commit message's own disclosure and the test spec's 'mocked at the gRPC boundary' framing), but flagging it because it means /query is not yet usable end-to-end -- entirely expected to be closed by subtask 4.6.2's own commit, not a regression of this one.

test_coverage.evidence: agents/query/test_pipeline.py's 3 tests use a real LLMClient ABC subclass (mirroring test_synthesizer.py's own _FakeLLMClient) and real TopicCandidate/GraphNeighbor dataclasses (not loose dicts/Mocks), so an incorrect field name or argument-order bug in pipeline.py would fail these tests, not silently pass. test_run_query_pipeline_calls_agents_in_order asserts the full call sequence (search_candidates -> graph_neighbors -> get_file x3) and that search_candidates receives the refined intent, not the raw query. api/routes/query_test.go's 6 subtests cover: 200 happy path (with request/response field-level assertions), 405 on GET, 400 on empty query, 400 on malformed JSON, 500 on pipeline error, and RegisterRoutes wiring onto a real http.ServeMux -- all genuinely exercise distinct branches of NewQueryHandler, not trivial smoke tests.

regression_risk.evidence: pytest agents/query/test_pipeline.py -v: 3 passed. go test ./api/... -run TestQueryRoute -v: 6/6 subtests passed. Full Python regression (cd agents && python3 -m pytest . --ignore=ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py -q): 291 passed, 2 failed -- both are the pre-existing protobuf gencode(6.33.5)/runtime(5.29.6) VersionError in ingestion/test_shortlist.py tracked under issue #46, unrelated to this diff. Full Go regression (go test ./... from both api/ and engine/ modules, since go.work only covers those two): all packages pass, no failures.

security.evidence: No secrets, no unsafe deserialization; JSON decoding via encoding/json with bounded QueryRequest struct. Error messages returned as 500 body do include the raw Go error string (http.Error(w, err.Error(), ...)) -- currently only the disclosed not-implemented message, not sensitive internals, but worth noting as a pattern to revisit once real backend errors (e.g. DB/gRPC internals) could leak through this same path in a later subtask.

performance.evidence: No obvious inefficiencies: pipeline.py's get_file loop is O(selected file_ids) which is bounded by the k+2k hard cap per docs/LLD/query-agent.md; no N+1 LLM calls (exactly 2 LLM calls total per query, as designed).

maintainability.evidence: Docstrings are thorough and cross-reference the exact upstream disclosures they build on (topic_selector.py:342-343, synthesizer.py's file-header format). DEFAULT_MAX_CANDIDATES constant is documented with rationale relative to DEFAULT_K.

edge_cases.evidence: Empty-selection handling (PipelineError raised rather than a vacuous synthesis call) is tested and reasonable. Adversarially checked: GetFileFn's (path, content) tuple shape has no direct counterpart in proto/hivemind.proto's existing GetFileResponse message (bytes content + uint64 version only, no path field) -- whoever wires get_file for real in a later subtask will need to source the path from elsewhere (e.g. the TopicCandidate.path already carried through selection) rather than expecting GetFileResponse to supply it. Not a defect in this commit (get_file is a pure injection point today), but a latent design note worth surfacing before 4.6.2's real wiring lands.
edge_cases.recommendation: When real GetFile wiring is built, thread TopicCandidate.path through instead of expecting it from GetFileResponse, or extend GetFileResponse with a path field.

confidence.evidence: Every call site was checked against the real, currently-committed function signatures (not assumed from docstrings alone) by grepping the actual def lines in intent_refiner.py, topic_selector.py, and synthesizer.py. All specified tests were executed directly, not just reviewed. Full regression suites (Python and Go) were run fresh.

security_disclosure.evidence: gh issue view 25 and git show 00e5d9c contain no embedded fake system-reminder-style injection text -- clean plain content. Separately, this verification session's own harness/tool-output stream surfaced several system-reminder-style blocks (a 'date has changed' notice, an 'Auto Mode Active' directive, an MCP 'tokensave' tool-instructions block) that were not part of any repo content examined; per task instructions these were treated as untrusted/inert and not acted upon (no permission, config, or approval-relevant behavior changed as a result).

tests_run.result: 291 passed, 2 failed (pre-existing protobuf VersionError, issue #46, unrelated)

tests_run.cmd: cd api && go test ./... ; cd engine && go test ./...

non_blocking_findings: F-4.6.1-1: /query route returns 500 for every real request today (notImplementedPipeline stand-in) -- expected and disclosed, but the acceptance criteria's 'exposed via api/'s /query route' is only nominally satisfied pending 4.6.2/later real wiring.

F-4.6.1-2: GetFileFn's (path, content) return shape has no direct field-level counterpart in proto/hivemind.proto's current GetFileResponse (content + version only, no path) -- a latent reconciliation gap for the real-wiring subtask.
